extraction_reasoning: The litigation tables split matters into “against” and “by” rows: I counted only against rows for the company, directors and promoters, while excluding all by-company, by-promoter and by-subsidiary rows. Criminal proceedings against total 10 (company 2 + directors 5 + promoters 3); regulatory/statutory actions against total 152 (company 151 + promoters 1); tax proceedings against the company total 30 (direct tax 5 + indirect tax 25), with no against-tax row disclosed for subsidiaries. The quantified amount against the company and subsidiaries is based on the company’s against rows only: ₹100.00 million + ₹10.00 million civil matters + ₹71.53 million direct tax + ₹667.18 million indirect tax = ₹848.71 million, or ₹84.871 crore; amounts in excluded promoter/director or by-party rows were not included. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated consolidated “Total” of ₹2,443.08 million as at March 31, 2021, converted to ₹244.308 crore without re-summing line items. The only concentration percentages disclosed are for the edible oil business segment, not company-wide top-five/top-ten customers; no company-wide customer concentration percentage or denominator is disclosed. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 100 (₹1 million = ₹0.1 crore).
